questions.question: What is a default interface method?
options: A) A method that can be overridden by a class implementation.
B) A method that can only be implemented by a class implementation.
C) A method that can be implemented by a class implementation but is optional.
D) A method that can only be implemented by an interface.
questions.explanation: A default interface method is a method that a class must implement, but can choose to provide its own implementation. It is denoted by the 'default' keyword.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a static method in an interface?
options: A) To be used by a class implementation.
B) To be used by the interface itself.
C) To be used by other interfaces.
D) To be used by other classes.
questions.explanation: A static method in an interface is used by the interface itself, rather than by a class implementation. It is a way to provide utility methods that can be used without creating an instance of the interface.

questions.question: Can a private method be a part of an interface?
options: A) Yes, it can be a part of an interface.
B) No, it cannot be a part of an interface.
C) It depends on the context.
D) It is not allowed in Java.
E) It is allowed in Java but can only be used once in the interface.
questions.explanation: No, a private method cannot be a part of an interface in Java. Private methods are not accessible from outside the class, and interfaces can only provide public methods.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'super' keyword in a class that implements an interface?
options: A) To call the superclass constructor.
B) To call the interface method.
C) To call the interface method and the superclass method.
D) To ignore the interface method.
questions.explanation: The 'super' keyword is used to call the superclass constructor from a child class that implements an interface.

questions.question: Can a class implement multiple interfaces?
options: A) Yes, it can implement multiple interfaces.
B) No, it cannot implement multiple interfaces.
C) It depends on the context.
D) It is allowed in Java.
E) It is not allowed in Java but can be implemented using a multilevel hierarchy.
questions.explanation: Yes, a class can implement multiple interfaces in Java. This is known as multilevel inheritance.
